role_summary: Mississippi voter-registration organizer; co-founder of the Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party; survivor of the June 9, 1963 Winona jail beating; the 1964 Democratic National Convention witness whose televised testimony reshaped the party-credentials challenge.

photo.alt: Fannie Lou Hamer at the Democratic National Convention, Atlantic City, August 22, 1964
photo.photographer: Warren K. Leffler, U.S. News & World Report Magazine
photo.repository: Wikimedia Commons (via Library of Congress Prints and Photographs Division)
photo.license: Public domain (Library of Congress: no known copyright restrictions; U.S. News & World Report collection donated to LoC)

ai_reading: Eight corpus voices discuss Hamer, the largest in-corpus discussion footprint of any external figure tracked here: Lisa Anderson Todd (entry 79), Gwendolyn M. Patton (entry 52), the Ladner sisters (entry 75), Euvester Simpson (entry 41, who was beaten with Hamer at the Winona jail in June 1963), Cleveland Sellers (entry 22), Walter Bruce (entry 123), William Lamar Strickland (entry 127), and Courtland Cox (entry 24, the MFDP convention-challenge logistics organizer). The Euvester Simpson cosine is particularly significant: the embedding aligns the survivor-witness testimony with the figure she witnessed.
biographical_paragraph: Fannie Lou Hamer is discussed in this corpus by 8 interviewees across the catalog (Lisa Anderson Todd (entry 79), Gwendolyn M. Patton (entry 52), Ladners joint interview (Dorie Ann Ladner + Joyce Ann Ladner) (entry 75), Euvester Simpson (entry 41), Dr. Cleveland Sellers (entry 22), Walter Bruce (entry 123), William Lamar Strickland (entry 127), Courtland Cox (entry 24)), but was not interviewed for the Civil Rights History Project; this catalog page treats Fannie Lou Hamer as an external figure whose presence in the archive is mediated through other voices. The canonical scholarly source the catalog cites for biographical orientation appears as [src: 1] below.

sources.title: Chana Kai Lee, For Freedom's Sake: The Life of Fannie Lou Hamer
sources.publisher: University of Illinois Press, 1999; WorldCat OCLC 41257260
